title: Today's Tech Brief: Brain Chips and AI Apps
summary: China approves world's first invasive brain-computer chip and Sekai raises $20M for AI app creation.

body.text: AI App Creation on the Rise

In other news, Sekai, a company focused on AI app creation, has raised $20 million in funding. This brings to light the growing interest in AI-powered applications and the potential for new platforms and infrastructure to support this growth. It's exciting to see where this investment will take the industry. With more companies like Sekai working on AI app creation, we can expect to see some innovative solutions emerge.

And there's more - I've been following another story about brain-computer interfaces, and it's amazing to see how far they've come. From controlling devices to potential treatments for neurological disorders, the possibilities are vast. It's a thrilling time for tech, and I'm excited to see what the future holds.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
